ddls:
CREATE TABLE "change_requests" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change request */,
  "change_number" TEXT /* Unique change number assigned to the request */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was requested */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who requested the change */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was reviewed */,
  "approval_status" TEXT /* Status of the change approval process */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (minor edit, major revision, new document addition) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the change request */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the change request */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* User ID of the person assigned to review or approve the change request */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Deadline for the change request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_requests_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the author belongs to */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department ID of the author */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Manager ID of the author */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_authors_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_details" (
  "change_detail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change detail */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change request */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (minor edit, major revision, new document addition) */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file associated with the change */,
  "file_content" TEXT /* Actual file content or a link to the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_detail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_details_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES change_requests ("change_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change history entry */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change request */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_number" TEXT /* Unique change number assigned to the request */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (minor edit, major revision, new document addition) */,
  "previous_version" TEXT /* Previous version of the document */,
  "new_version" TEXT /* New version of the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_history_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES change_requests ("change_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documents" (
  "document_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "document_name" TEXT /* Name of the document */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the document */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the document was last updated */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of document (playbook, guideline, operational procedure) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the document */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Keywords for the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("document_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_document_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change request */,
  "document_id" INTEGER /* ID of the document affected by the change */,
  "mapping_type" TEXT /* Type of mapping (one-to-one, one-to-many) */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the mapping becomes effective */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_document_mapping_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES change_requests ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_document_mapping_document_id FOREIGN KEY ("document_id") REFERENCES documents ("document_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., change initiator, reviewer, approver, admin) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role ID of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change request being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the change request */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the change request was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (view, edit, approve) */,
  "access_result" TEXT /* Result of the access attempt (success, failure, error) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments about the access attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES change_requests ("change_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
